sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Row 3 col 1 didn't move any shape! The shapes stayed at their original positions. So row 3 doesn't control any movement for col 1.
Let me summarize what I know so far:
- Row 1 col 1: shape 2 moves LEFT (rejected)
- Row 2 col 1: shape 2 moves RIGHT (rejected)
- Row 3 col 1: no movement
Maybe rows 4-6 control shape 1 or other movements. Let me test them quickly.
But actually, I'm spending way too many actions on probing. I've used 144 actions and I'm still on level 2. I need to be much more efficient.
Let me think about this differently. The left panel has row 1 all active (B B B B). This is the "target" bar pattern. The right panel bars need to match this pattern.
When I set the right panel row 1 to all active, shape 2 moves left and returns. The level doesn't complete.
Maybe the issue is that I need to match the LEFT panel bar pattern on the RIGHT panel, but the left panel bars also need to be in a specific state.
Or maybe the game is about moving the shapes to match the left panel target shape, and the bars control the movement. The left panel bars show the "correct" movement pattern.
Let me try a completely different approach. Instead of testing individual bars, let me try to match the left panel bar pattern exactly on the right panel. The left panel has:
- Row 1: B B B B
- Rows 2-6: w w w w
I already tried this, but let me try again with a fresh state. First, I need to reset all right panel bars to w, then set row 1 to all B.
Actually wait, let me check the current bar state first.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Row 1=LEFT, Row 2=RIGHT for shape 2, Row 3=no movement. Need to stop probing and commit to a plan. Left panel has row 1 all active as target pattern.
Plan: Reset all bars, then set right panel row 1 to match left panel (all B), click blue circle. If this doesn't work, try matching all 6 rows.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current bar state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
active_bars = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    if 32 <= min_r <= 50 and 34 <= min_c <= 63 and n['pixels'] <= 5:
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        if n['color'] == 'B':
            active_bars.append((cr, cc))
print(f"Active right panel bars: {active_bars}")

# Reset all active bars, then set row 1 to all B
# Current active: row 3 col 1 (39, 39)
reset_actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(r), 'col': int(c)} for r, c in active_bars]
set_row1_actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': c} for c in [39, 44, 49, 54]]
blue_circle = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 46}]

action(reset_actions + set_row1_actions + blue_circle)

# Check result
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
if last_animation:
    for entry in last_animation:
        if len(entry.frames) > 1:
            print(f"Blue circle: {len(entry.frames)} frames")
            for j, frame in enumerate(entry.frames):
                seg = frame.segmentation
                y_shapes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 10]
                if y_shapes:
                    centers = [(round(sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']), 1), 
                               round(sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']), 1)) for n in y_shapes]
                    print(f"  Frame {j}: {centers}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
